Acta v0.2.0-pilot record · Generated from accepted canonical state (explanations/tag-filter-xray.md) after explicit acceptance (chat 2026-07-23 — explanation verified against the five source files listed as inputs) · Never generated from browser state.

dossier record · ACCEPTED

How tag filtering works in the skills catalog

URL-derived rendering over a pure functional core: one parameter, two pure functions, one DOM sink. Verified against source at a8b0837 — every claim carries its file:line location.

TL;DR

Verified against source

Every render is derived from the URL: parseTagFromSearch reads the single ?tag= parameter, filterByTag narrows visibleSkills() by inclusion, and app.mjs replaces the list DOM. Without JavaScript the full catalog with plain ?tag= chip links is already complete — the script only enhances it.

System map

Four small modules, one dependency direction: the DOM depends on pure functions, never the reverse.

Solid edges run on every navigation and popstate. The dark node is the only mutable surface in the feature.

The operational path, step by step

01Identify the requested tagsrc/url-tag.mjs:6

parseTagFromSearch(location.search) reads the single ?tag= parameter via URLSearchParams and returns the tag string or null. Duplicate parameters resolve to the first occurrence; there is deliberately no multi-tag syntax.

02Narrow the catalogsrc/catalog.mjs:8

filterByTag(skills, tag) returns the full list for a null tag and filters by tags.includes(tag) otherwise, preserving input order. It is a plain export — node --test imports it directly.

03Re-derive the DOMsrc/app.mjs:14

renderFromUrl() pipes the two pure functions and calls replaceChildren with either the visible rows or — when a tag is present and the list is empty — the accepted explain-and-suggest empty state.

04History traversal replays the same pathsrc/app.mjs:27

popstate re-invokes renderFromUrl; there is no separate history handler, so back/forward can never diverge from a fresh load of the same URL.

Using it

Adding a taggable skill requires no middleware knowledge — tags live next to the skill entry and the chips derive from them.

src/catalog.mjs (excerpt)
export function visibleSkills() {
  return [
    { name: "Changed-file summarizer", tags: ["git", "review"] },
    { name: "Test-gap finder", tags: ["tests", "review"] },
    // add a new entry here — its tags become filter chips automatically
  ];
}

Gotchas worth knowing

  • The no-JS page is the baseline, not a fallback. index.html ships the complete catalog with working ?tag= links; app.mjs enhances it after load. Testing with JavaScript disabled exercises a real supported path.
  • A missing tag and an unknown tag are different states. null (no ?tag=) renders the full catalog; an unknown tag renders the empty state. Collapsing the two was the root cause of incident INC-0721.
  • Order stability is asserted, not assumed. filterByTag preserves visibleSkills() order by construction, and test/catalog.test.mjs pins it — reorderings are test failures, not surprises.

FAQ

How do I add a new tag?

Add it to any skill's tags array — chips and filters derive from the data; nothing else changes.

Can users filter by two tags?

No — multi-tag was an explicit Gate A non-goal. The URL schema (single ?tag=) would need a new spec round.

Where do I see what renders for an unknown tag?

Open any ?tag= value that no skill carries; the accepted empty state (interface-directions, direction B) renders with the real tag set as chips.

Sources

  1. [E1] src/catalog.mjs — filterByTag, visibleSkills
  2. [E2] src/url-tag.mjs — parseTagFromSearch
  3. [E3] src/app.mjs — browser glue
  4. [E4] index.html — static catalog markup
  5. [E5] test/catalog.test.mjs — behavior assertions

Acta is a print-first engineering record. Fact = directly supported; Inference = reasoned from evidence; Simplification = deliberately reduced model. This record renders accepted canonical state only; open decisions live in the companion instrument.
